_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
source_files: Diagnostic Hysteroscopy and Hysteroscopic IUCD Removal_Medco.pdf
Diagnostic Hysteroscopy and Hysteroscopic IUCD Removal_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Selected infertility cases having abnormal hysterography
Congenital malformation of the uterus (septate, bicornuate)

preauth.label: Detailed clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indications, and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes all vitals, detailed history especially medication history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment and advice for admission are present?
Did the history, clinical examination, and investigation/imaging confirm the diagnosis?
Clinical presentation was indicative of hysteroscopy?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging/investigation indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation not indicative of hysteroscopy
Detailed clinical notes are missing or incomplete
Hemoglobin/Complete blood count results are missing or do not support the medical necessity of the procedure
Urine complete examination (CUE) report is missing or incomplete
USG abdomen/pelvis report is missing or does not support the diagnosis
Diagnostic hysteroscopy report is missing at the time of claim submission

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with minor operation theatre setting when procedure to be done under local anaesthesia; Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre with Anesthetist and Anesthesia facility when procedure to be done under general anaesthesia
clinical_key_pointers: Common presentation For Diagnostic Hysteroscopy: One of the following menstrual problems: Irregular vaginal bleeding
Common presentation For Diagnostic Hysteroscopy: Intermenstrual bleeding
Common presentation For Diagnostic Hysteroscopy: Amenorrhea following a vaginal procedure
Common presentation For Diagnostic Hysteroscopy: Unexplained infertility
Common presentation For Diagnostic Hysteroscopy: Recurrent pregnancy loss
Common presentation For Diagnostic Hysteroscopy: Post-menopausal bleeding
Common presentation For Diagnostic Hysteroscopy: Primary and secondary amenorrhea
complications: The most common complications are uterine perforation and bleeding.
Other more rare and morbid complications are air and CO2 embolisms, postoperative infections, and severe electrolyte disturbances.
